comingSoon.eyebrow: a small skin studio · kitsilano, vancouver
comingSoon.title: *coming* soon.
comingSoon.sub: we're opening the doors shortly. if you have the password, step inside.

hero.title:
your skin,
*considered.*
hero.sub: a small skin studio on west broadway. each visit begins with a slow read of your skin and ends with a plan that fits your week, your budget, and your patience.

meta.dd: face at a time, by appointment

philosophy.title:
slow skin care.
*honest* results.
philosophy.lede: we don't sell quick fixes. each appointment is a full hour at minimum, set aside for one person at a time. the work is unhurried, the conversation is private, and the products are chosen for what your skin actually needs — not what we have in stock.

triad.title: *read*
triad.body: every first visit begins with a careful skin reading — under good light, without a clock running. we ask about your routine, your history, and what brought you in.

triad.title: *treat*
triad.body: a treatment chosen for today's skin, not last month's plan. cleanse, exfoliation, modalities matched to your needs, and a soft lymphatic finish.

triad.title: *tend*
triad.body: you leave with a written plan and a small kit of samples. nothing is upsold. if you don't need a serum, we won't sell you a serum.

studioSplit.title: a single room. *one face* at a time.
studioSplit.body_p1_before_link: mei skin is a one-practitioner boutique built around the belief that good skin care is unhurried. set inside
studioSplit.body_p1_after_link: on west broadway, there's a single treatment room, a single appointment slot per hour, and a single person caring for your skin from start to finish.
studioSplit.body_p2: we work with plant-based and pharmaceutical-grade actives — no marketing buzzwords, no surprise add-ons, no upselling at the front desk. the prices on the menu are the prices you pay.
studioSplit.frame_label: a single room, soft window light

services.title: the menu.
services.note: the prices below are the prices you pay — no add-ons at the desk.

items.name: the mei reset facial (recovery + restoration)
items.description:
a boutique 90-minute restorative facial designed for skin that feels irritated, stressed, or easily reactive. 

the mei reset combines a spa experience with results reflecting the approach of skin clinics in Japan and South Korea, using the same medical-grade Korean formulations alongside advanced skin technologies.

this customized treatment focuses on soothing visible irritation, supporting barrier repair, and improving overall comfort and balance in the skin. Each session is composed to help reduce redness, increase moisture retention, and refine texture and tone.

your treatment may include ultrasonic skin scrubber extractions, eye/face RF lifting, plasma + high-frequency for collagen-building, and multi-modal circulation support, paired with professional-grade products from Dr. esthé, Éclat du teint, and Dermabell.

the result is calmer, clearer skin with enhanced barrier function, and a replenished complexion.

items.name: microneedling
items.description: collagen induction therapy for fine lines, acne scarring, and overall skin density. numbing, a precise pen depth, and gentle aftercare.

services.footnote: full menu including brow shaping, lash tinting, and body services available on request. members receive priority booking and pricing.

aboutMei.title: meet brrrriannnna
aboutMei.lede: skin care, for me, started with my own. a long stretch of trial and error, a handful of mentors, and the slow realization that the work i wanted to do wasn't in a chain spa — it was in a small room, with one person, taking the time. joean was here
aboutMei.body: i trained in clinical aesthetics in vancouver and spent six years on treatment-room floors at studios across the city before opening mei skin. i still see every visit the same way: it's an hour for you, and the only job is to leave your skin (and you) a little more at home in itself. philip was here
aboutMei.pull_quote: i won't sell you something you don't need. if your skin's doing well, i'll tell you to come back in eight weeks, not six.

atmosphere.title: an hour that's *actually* yours.
atmosphere.body: no rotating staff, no shared waiting room, no other faces in the building during your treatment. just one practitioner, one room, and the time to do the work properly.

items.name: philip
items.quote: i'd been to four other facialists this year before i found mei. the first visit changed how i think about my skin.

items.name: marcus
items.quote: my skin barrier was wrecked from over-exfoliating. she fixed in three visits what i'd been chasing for two years.

items.name: rosa
items.quote: i came in for a peel. she talked me out of it and recommended led + a different home routine instead. that kind of honesty is rare.

items.name: jun
items.quote: microcurrent + her hands. i'm forty-one and people keep asking what i've done. i tell them.

items.name: elena
items.quote: i've been a member for two years. my skin has never looked more like itself.

items.name: davis
items.quote: my partner gifted me a series for our anniversary. now we both go.

items.name: priya
items.quote: the consultation alone is worth the price of admission. i took notes.

items.name: theo
items.quote: i drive in from the north shore for every appointment. there's nowhere else in town i'd rather sit on a table.

items.name: the *studio*
items.address:
inside we do beauty studio
west broadway · kitsilano
vancouver, bc

items.q: what should i expect on my first visit?
items.a: we start with a careful skin reading — under good light, with no clock running. i ask about your routine, your history, and what brought you in. the treatment is chosen for what i see, not a pre-set menu. you'll leave with a written plan and a small kit of samples.

items.q: how long are appointments, and how often should i come?
items.a: first visits run 90 minutes; follow-ups are usually 60 or 75 depending on the treatment. cadence depends on your skin — most clients land somewhere between every four and eight weeks. i won't book you in more often than your skin needs.
i carry a small, curated shelf of skin care that i actually use on clients. i won't push it. if a drugstore product would work better for your case, i'll tell you which one and why.

items.q: i have sensitive or reactive skin. is this for me?
items.a: yes — and especially yes. a lot of clients arrive after their barrier has been worked over by harsh routines or rushed appointments elsewhere. we start gently, with calming modalities and a slow rebuild. patch tests are standard.

items.q: can i bring products i'm already using?
items.a: please. bring everything — including the things you're embarrassed about. we go through it together and figure out what stays, what goes, and what's missing. no judgment, ever.

24-hour notice for full credit. cancellations inside 24 hours are charged 50% of the booked service. i hold one slot per hour, so a late cancel means an empty room — i appreciate your help keeping it fair.
yes, in any amount. delivered electronically or as a small printed card. they don't expire.
street parking on west broadway and the side streets, free after 6pm. the studio is a short walk from broadway-city hall (10–12 min) or any bus along the broadway corridor.

seo.street_address: inside we do beauty studio, west broadway

newsletter.title: a quiet *note,* now and then.
newsletter.body: openings, new services, and the occasional skin care thought from mei. once a month, never more. unsubscribe whenever — we won't be offended.

footer.tagline: a boutique restorative skin studio. by appointment, kitsilano, vancouver.
